title: 10 possible side effects of Alchemilla mollis (lady's mantle)

side_effects_list.side_effect_desc: Alchemilla mollis causes allergic reactions when used. This is due to its ability to stimulate the immune system and trigger an overactive response in some individuals. The plant contains alkaloids and saponins, which can cause an allergic reaction when ingested or applied topically, leading to symptoms such as skin irritation, hives, and swelling.

Alchemilla mollis produces digestive issues when consumed. The plant contains tannins and flavonoids, which can cause stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea due to its ability to bind with proteins in the digestive tract and slow down digestion. Additionally, the plant's astringent properties may also contribute to dry mouth and throat irritation, exacerbating the discomfort caused by digestive issues.

Alchemilla mollis increases blood pressure due to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. These compounds stimulate the cardiovascular system, causing an increase in blood pressure by constricting blood vessels and increasing heart rate. Additionally, the herb's diuretic properties can further contribute to this effect, as it reduces blood volume and increases the force of contractions on the heart, ultimately leading to elevated blood pressure.

Alchemilla mollis triggers respiratory problems due to its potential allergenic and irritant properties. The plant contains alkaloids, flavonoids, and polyphenols that may cause an allergic reaction or irritation to the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract, leading to symptoms such as coughing, shortness of breath, and wheezing.

Alchemilla mollis exacerbates skin conditions due to its potential to cause allergic reactions and increase sensitivity. The plant's compounds can trigger histamine release, leading to inflammation and irritation of the skin. Additionally, Alchemilla mollis may contain irritant properties that can disrupt the natural barrier function of the skin, further exacerbating existing skin issues or inducing new ones.

Alchemilla mollis affects kidney function due to its potential to alter blood pressure and increase urine production. The plant contains compounds like flavonoids and triterpenoids that can interact with the kidneys' ability to filter waste products from the bloodstream, potentially leading to changes in creatinine levels or increased excretion of electrolytes. As a result, users may experience altered kidney function, which could be monitored through regular blood tests.

Alchemilla mollis interacts with medications that have a blood-thinning effect, such as warfarin and aspirin. This is because the herb contains compounds like alchemillol and its derivatives, which can enhance the activity of these medications, leading to an increased risk of bleeding and bruising.

Alchemilla mollis induces stomach ulcers. This is due to its ability to increase digestive secretions and reduce the natural protective lining of the stomach, making it more susceptible to irritation from acid and other digestive enzymes. Additionally, the herb's anti-inflammatory properties may contribute to the development of stomach ulcers by reducing blood flow to the stomach lining and impeding healing processes.

Alchemilla mollis impairs cognitive functions due to its ability to interfere with neurotransmitter receptors in the brain. Specifically, the plant contains flavonoids and triterpenoid saponins that can bind to GABA receptors, leading to a disruption of normal neuronal activity. This may result in impaired concentration, memory, and reaction time, making it challenging for individuals to perform daily tasks that require mental clarity.

Alchemilla mollis enhances bleeding risk due to its ability to increase menstrual flow and thin blood vessels. The plant contains compounds that stimulate uterine contractions, leading to increased menstrual discharge, which can prolong the period and increase bleeding. Additionally, Alchemilla mollis may reduce blood clotting factors, making it more susceptible to bleeding when injured or with surgery. This effect is particularly concerning for individuals taking anticoagulant medications or undergoing surgical procedures.

intro: Alchemilla mollis has some side effects when used improperly, such as gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with medications. The causes of these side effects are typically attributed to incorrect dosage, individual sensitivity, or combining it with other substances that can enhance its potency. Worsening one's life, frequent stomach cramps and diarrhea from improper usage can disrupt daily routines and social activities, while severe allergic reactions can lead to anxiety, breathing difficulties, and even hospitalization.
intro_desc: Alchemilla mollis has some side effects when used improperly, such as gastrointestinal upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with medications. These side effects can occur due to excessive consumption or allergic sensitization. For example, people who experience severe stomach cramps, diarrhea, or difficulty breathing after using lady's mantle may have to take time off work, skip important social events, or avoid their favorite foods.
